sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"M block: center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- From (36, 18) to (34, 18): clicked (35, 19), distance from block center to click = ~1.4, block moved 2 up, 0 right
- From (34, 18) to (32, 20): clicked (32, 20), distance = ~2.8, block moved 2 up, 2 right
- From (32, 20) to (30, 22): clicked (30, 22), distance = ~2.8, block moved 2 up, 2 right
So it seems like blocks can move up to about 2-3 cells per click. Let me try to move the blocks closer to the blue blob more efficiently.
The blue blob is at (27, 33). The block is now at (30, 22). I need to move it to approximately (25, 33) to be above the blob.
Let me try clicking further away to see if the block can move more than 2 cells.
